data.content: The file is generated successfully but never added to the compilation. The target needs to include the generated file in the `Compile` item group so the C# compiler picks it up.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Add the generated file to the compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The file is written to disk but the compiler doesn't know about it.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` items within the target, using `<ItemGroup>` inside the target:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This needs to be added after the `WriteCodeFile` task call in the `GenerateSampleCode` target.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Add the generated file to the compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never added it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's C# compiler only compiles files listed in that group, so the generated file was silently ignored.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target, after the file is written, so the compiler picks it up.
